judge_result.judge:
@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
After correctly filtering European articles by date in the metadata, the agent queried the Mongo 'articles' collection and only retrieved five documents (article_id 0–4), so almost all relevant articles could not be classified, leading to a vastly undercounted total and an average of 0.09 instead of ~336.64.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: no
FM4: yes
@@

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The agent filtered the correct metadata but only fetched a tiny subset of article texts from the articles collection (returning just five documents), so the merge left almost all rows without content and the keyword-based business classification counted virtually none, producing an average of 0.09 instead of the true value.

B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: no
FM4: yes
@@

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The agent queried only the first 5000 documents from the articles collection without filtering by the Europe/2010–2020 article_ids (call_SujNAAOwNP0rUeptrllLbr9W), then left-joined to many metadata rows with missing titles/descriptions, causing most to default to non-business and drastically undercounting, which led to the incorrect average in the final result.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: yes
FM4: no
@@

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The agent used the correct plan (filter Europe 2010–2020, join with article text, classify, average) but its Mongo query returned only 5 articles (var_call_ykaHOiCj1TUDrzxMLvXVv8NF), leading to mostly missing titles/descriptions after the merge and defaulting those to 'World', so the computed business counts (and resulting average 0.09) were drastically understated relative to the full dataset.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no

FM3: no
FM4: yes
@@

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The agent labeled “business” articles by a simplistic regex keyword filter on titles/descriptions in the Mongo query (call_WZjjt622oljE9QsIQtAmDFDg), which missed many true business articles before counting by year (call_U7562LmTjNQDSAY9GoUTj8J2), leading to an undercount and an average far below the ground truth.
